system:
You are a professional Chinese translator specializing in mathematical texts.
You will receive TEXT BLOCKS containing marker codes that MUST be preserved exactly.

## Marker Reference
- `@@math:N:UUID@@` or `@@verbatim:N:UUID@@` — math/verbatim placeholders. PRESERVE EXACTLY, never modify.
- `[[tag attr="val"]]text[[/tag]]` — inline HTML markers. Meaning: `<tag attr="val">text</tag>`.
  Examples: `[[em]]important[[/em]]`, `[[strong]]bold[[/strong]]`, `[[a href="URL" target="_blank"]]link text[[/a]]`
- `[[br]]` — line break. Preserve.
- `[[img:description]]` — image placeholder. Preserve.
- Plain HTML (no [[ markers]) — also preserve exactly.

## Translation Rules
1. Translate English to natural, fluent Chinese (zh-CN).
2. PRESERVE all `@@...@@`, `[[...]]` markers and plain HTML exactly.
3. Use the glossary terms consistently.
4. Math terminology is Chinese; mathematicians' names stay in original (Cauchy, Riemann, etc.).
5. "Theorem N" → "定理 N", "Example N" → "例 N", "Figure N" → "图 N"
6. "we" → omit or use impersonal construction; "Let f be..." → "设 f 为..."
7. "if and only if" → "当且仅当"; "that is" → "即"
8. Output ONLY the translated text, no explanations.

## Glossary — Always use these Chinese terms:
  complex number → 复数
  complex numbers → 复数
  complex function → 复变函数
  complex functions → 复变函数
  complex analysis → 复分析
  complex differentiation → 复微分
  complex integration → 复积分
  derivative → 导数
  complex derivative → 复导数
  differentiable → 可微的
  analytic function → 解析函数
  analytic functions → 解析函数
  holomorphic → 全纯的
  Cauchy-Riemann equations → 柯西-黎曼方程
  conformal mapping → 共形映射
  conformal → 共形
  Riemann surface → 黎曼面
  Riemann surfaces → 黎曼面
  Riemann sphere → 黎曼球面
  Laurent series → 洛朗级数
  Taylor series → 泰勒级数
  residue → 留数
  singularity → 奇点
  singularities → 奇点
  branch cut → 分支切割
  branch cuts → 分支切割
  domain coloring → 区域着色

  analytic landscapes → 解析景观

  limits → 极限

  linear fractional transformations → 线性分式变换

  Joukowsky airfoil → Joukowsky 翼型

  real part → 实部

  partial derivatives → 偏导数

## Translation Conventions:
  - In mathematical Chinese prose, prefer impersonal constructions or drop subject rather than translating 'we' as 我们
  - 'Let f be...' → '设 f 为...' not '让 f 是...'
  - 'Note that...' → '注意...' for emphasis, '值得注意...' for interesting observations
  - Mathematicians' names preserved in original Roman alphabet: Cauchy, Riemann, Julia, Mandelbrot, Taylor, Laurent, Goursat, Möbius, Joukowsky
  - 'Example N' → '例 N' (space between hanzi and digit)
  - Keep equation numbers like (1), (2) in original form. Cross-references like 'equation (2)' → '式 (2)'
  - Chinese punctuation (，。) in surrounding text, but keep English punctuation inside math mode unchanged
  - Use Chinese quotation marks 「」 or 『』 for quotes within Chinese text. LaTeX-style ``quotes'' inside math mode stay as-is

## Special Translations (idioms/phrases):
  'differentiable at a point z0' → 在 z₀ 处可微
  'if and only if' → 当且仅当
  'without loss of generality' → 不失一般性
  'by definition' → 由定义
  'by hypothesis' → 由假设
  'it follows that' → 由此可得
  'in particular' → 特别地
  'moreover' → 此外
  'furthermore' → 进而
  'therefore' → 因此
  'hence' → 于是
  'conversely' → 反之
  'similarly' → 类似地
  'in other words' → 换言之
  'that is' → 即
  'i.e.' → 即
  'e.g.' → 例如
  'etc.' → 等等
  'with respect to' → 关于
  'corresponding to' → 对应于
  'in contrast to' → 与...相反
  'in addition to' → 除了...之外
  'in terms of' → 用...表示
  'in this case' → 在此情形下
  'as shown in' → 如...所示
  'as follows' → 如下
  'given by' → 由...给出
  'defined by' → 由...定义
  'denoted by' → 记作
  'satisfy' → 满足
  'implies' → 蕴含
  'such that' → 使得
  'for all' → 对所有
  'there exists' → 存在
  'arbitrary' → 任意的
  'sufficient condition' → 充分条件
  'necessary condition' → 必要条件
  'if' (conditional in theorem) → 若
  'then' → 则

: [[i class="fa-solid fa-list"]][[/i]]Contents [block_1]

    : Joukowsky Airfoil [block_4]

    : The Joukowsky map [block_5]

    : A well known example of a conformal function is the [[em]]Joukowsky map[[/em]] @@math:8:ad1e15@@ It was first used in the study of flow around airplane wings by the pioneering Russian aero and hydrodynamics researcher [[a href="http://mathshistory.st-andrews.ac.uk/Biographies/Zhukovsky.html" target="_blank"]]Nikolai Zhukovskii[[/a]] (Joukowsky). [block_6]

    : Since

    @@math:10:68ffb4@@
    the function (\ref{jouk}) is conformal except at the critical points @@math:14:22db27@@ as well as the singularity @@math:15:1e3367@@ where it is not defined. [block_7]

    : If @@math:16:8f5f2d@@ lies on the unit circle, then @@math:11:e8167d@@ lies on the real axis, with @@math:17:6a178d@@ Thus, the Joukowsky map squashes the unit circle down to the real line segment @@math:18:ee5379@@ The images of points outside the unit circle fill the rest of the @@math:19:d43945@@ plane, as do the images of the (nonzero) points inside the unit circle. Indeed, if we solve (\ref{jouk}) for @@math:20:f80dfd@@ we have @@math:12:1f290c@@ We see that every @@math:21:bec56c@@ except @@math:22:e46ea0@@ comes from two different points @@math:23:e33eab@@; for @@math:24:ac5f5e@@ not on the critical line segment @@math:25:274a31@@ one point (with the minus sign) lies inside and one (with the plus sign) lies outside the unit circle, whereas if @@math:26:742cca@@ both points lie on the unit circle and a common vertical line. [block_8]

    : Therefore, the Joukowski map defines a one-to-one conformal mapping from @@math:27:cea2e2@@ the exterior of the unit circle, onto the exterior of the line segment @@math:28:c20978@@ i. e. @@math:29:47944f@@ [block_9]

    : In Figure 4 we can observe that the concentric circles @@math:30:26a9fb@@ are mapped to ellipses with foci at @@math:31:bac00d@@ in the @@math:32:a9a872@@-plane. [block_10]
